entries.title: Curtains Turn Shibuya Into A Mission Zone

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: The Shibuya Incident begins as curtains and coordinated enemy plans turn a civilian district into a trapped mission zone for sorcerers.
content.injection: During Shibuya scenes, treat the area as a live sealed mission zone with civilians, barriers, and coordinated enemy pressure, not as an ordinary open battlefield.
constraints: Do not import post-Shibuya legal or Culling Game consequences before the aftermath anchor.
antiLore: Do not write Shibuya as a clean sorcerer-only arena.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Disaster Curses Coordinate To Seal Gojo

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: The enemy side's Shibuya plan is coordinated around neutralizing Gojo, with the Prison Realm as the decisive sealing tool.
content.injection: Before the sealing anchor, let enemy movement in Shibuya feel organized around isolating and sealing Gojo rather than simply fighting sorcerers at random.
constraints: Do not state that Gojo is already sealed before the sealing anchor.
antiLore: Do not write the disaster curses as attacking without a specific anti-Gojo objective.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Gojo's Sealing Changes Every Battlefield Priority

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: Once Gojo is sealed in the Prison Realm, Shibuya's tactical priority shifts from relying on him to surviving without the strongest sorcerer available.
content.injection: After the Gojo sealing anchor, write sorcerer decisions around the shock and practical vacuum of Gojo being unavailable.
constraints: Before the sealing anchor, do not treat Gojo as unavailable.
Do not import later unsealing outcomes from Culling Game or Shinjuku decks.
antiLore: Do not let Gojo freely resolve post-seal Shibuya scenes.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Mechamaru Contingency Guides Yuji After The Seal

coordinates.label: Yuji is responding after Gojo's seal

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: After Gojo's sealing, Mechamaru's contingency helps communicate the emergency and guide Yuji through the Shibuya chaos.
content.injection: When the Mechamaru contingency is in Context, use it as a narrow post-seal coordination aid rather than making every sorcerer omniscient about Shibuya.
constraints: Do not let the contingency erase confusion or communication breakdown across the whole incident.
antiLore: Do not treat post-seal Shibuya coordination as clean or centrally controlled.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.
